mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances when UNRATE crosses the threshold, or t-statistic for mean return difference is insignificant with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in new home sales and housing starts indicate a collapsing residential investment cycle, which reduces construction sector revenues and wealth effects, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint negative growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 across rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate capacity utilization, which supports earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during economic expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns during expansion phases, or the signal reverses direction with negative mean returns in > 60% of expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms broad-based economic expansion and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days following data releases.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistical relationship with 5-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 over consecutive 2-year evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above the 3.5 percent threshold forces monetary authorities to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and compressing equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL readings above 3.5% fail to precede negative 60-day returns, or the predictive accuracy drops below 50% in out-of-sample testing across multiple inflation cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations to drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal yields negative 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered cases, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with p > 0.05 in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining new home sales signal weakening residential demand and reduced household wealth, which directly suppresses consumer discretionary spending and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days during housing corrections.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines fail to predict negative 5-day returns during housing downturns, or the signal produces positive mean returns in > 60% of correction periods.
